template_object.comment: VERSIONING: used global constraint on modified datetime using generation config field. See below.
template_object.comment 2: 'apply_to': 'all' means that the given date constraint applies to ALL nodes involved, incuding intermediary
template_object.comment 3: 'apply_to': 'any' means that the given date constraint applies to AT LEAST one node involved in the query path solution, incuding intermediary nodoes

modified_constraints.comment: after, before, or none bay be omitted

template_object.order_by_ALT: other options: var.prop, var.prop, ..., random, none
template_object.text:
Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question: How could course of action {coa1:course-of-action.name} (id {coa1.id}) prevent achieving tactic {ta1.name} (id {ta1.id})?
Answer: mitigation or tactic {coa1.name} mitigates attack pattern {ap1:coa1.mitigates.name}, which achieves tactic {ta1:ap1.achieves.name} (id {ta1.id}).
Modification times: 
coa1: {coa1.modified}
ap1: {ap1.modified}
ta1: {ta1.modified}.
template_object.text-OLD:
Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question: How could course of action {coa1:course-of-action.name} (id {coa1.id}) prevent achieving tactic {ta1.name} (id {ta1.id})?
Answer: mitigation or tactic {coa1.name} mitigates attack pattern {ap1:coa1.mitigates.name}, which achieves tactic {ta1:ap1.achieves.name} (id {ta1.id}).
Modification times: 
coa1: {coa1.modified}
ap1: {ap1.modified}
ta1: {ta1.modified}. <*{coa1.modified[_>=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z]}  {ap1.modified[_>=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z]} {ta1.modified[_>=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z]} *>
template_object.text-short: Tool {tl:tool.name} uses technique {t1:tl.uses.name} and technique {t2:tl.uses.name}, both mitigated by mitigations (same!) {coa1:t1.mitigates<.name} / {coa2:t2.mitigates<.name}.

query:
MATCH (coa1:`course-of-action`), (ta1:`x-mitre-tactic`), (coa1:`course-of-action`)-[:mitigates]->(ap1:`attack-pattern`), (ap1:`attack-pattern`)-[:achieves]->(ta1:`x-mitre-tactic`), (ap1:`attack-pattern`)

LIMIT 10000
RETURN DISTINCT coa1.name, coa1.public_id, ta1.name, ta1.public_id, ap1.name, coa1.modified, ap1.modified, ta1.modified
ORDER BY rand()
